name: CSCOPXY
description: XY scope for plotting Y vs X with event-triggered refresh

descriptions: Number of XY curves to display

realParameters.description: Axis limits: xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax

integerParameters.description: Display config: ID, auto-flag, buffer, color, style, thickness flags, grid, axis flags, window size

controls.description: Event trigger for sampling and display refresh

xmlExample:
<BasicBlock id="b1" parent="0:2:0" interfaceFunctionName="CSCOPXY" blockType="d" dependsOnU="0" dependsOnT="0" simulationFunctionName="cscopxy" simulationFunctionType="C_OR_FORTRAN" style="CSCOPXY">
  <ScilabString as="exprs" height="11" width="1">
    <data line="0" column="0" value="1"/>
    <data line="1" column="0" value="4"/>
    <data line="2" column="0" value="1"/>
    <data line="3" column="0" value="-1"/>
    <data line="4" column="0" value="[]"/>
    <data line="5" column="0" value="[600;400]"/>
    <data line="6" column="0" value="-15"/>
    <data line="7" column="0" value="15"/>
    <data line="8" column="0" value="-15"/>
    <data line="9" column="0" value="15"/>
    <data line="10" column="0" value="2"/>
  </ScilabString>
  <mxGeometry as="geometry" x="300.0" y="200.0" width="40.0" height="40.0"/>
</BasicBlock>
anomalies: Two explicit inputs (X and Y) vs CSCOPE's one
Similar to CANIMXY but with different parameter ordering
criticalRules: Input 1 is X coordinate, Input 2 is Y coordinate
Event on ControlPort samples new (X,Y) point
Plots Y vs X (not Y vs time like CSCOPE)
Uses circular buffer for trajectory storage
